questions.question_text: A, B and C started a business by investing ‚āĻ 120000, ‚āĻ 135000 and ‚āĻ 150000 respectively. Find the share of each, out of an annual profit of ‚āĻ 56700.
questions.solution:
Ratio of shares of A, B and C = Ratio of their investments
= 120000 : 135000 : 150000 = 8 : 9 : 10.
‚ąī A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(56700\times\frac{8}{27}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 16800; B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(56700\times\frac{9}{27}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 18900;
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(56700\times\frac{10}{27}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 21000.

questions.question_text: Alfred started a business investing ‚āĻ 45000. After 3 months, Peter joined him with a capital of ‚āĻ 60000. After another 6 months, Ronald joined them with a capital of ‚āĻ 90000. At the end of the year, they made a profit of ‚āĻ 16500. Find the share of each.
questions.solution:
Clearly, Alfred invested his capital for 12 months, Peter for 9 months and Ronald for 3 months.
So, ratio of their capitals = (45000 $\times$ 12) : (60000 $\times$ 9) : (90000 $\times$ 3)
= 540000 : 540000 : 270000 = 2 : 2 : 1.
‚ąī Alfred‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(16500\times\frac{2}{5}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 6600; Peter‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(16500\times\frac{2}{5}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 6600;
Ronald‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(16500\times\frac{1}{5}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 3300.

questions.question_text: A, B and C start a business each investing ‚āĻ 20000. After 5 months A withdrew ‚āĻ 5000, B withdrew ‚āĻ 4000 and C invests ‚āĻ 6000 more. At the end of the year, a total profit of ‚āĻ 69900 was recorded. Find the share of each.
questions.solution:
Ratio of the capitals of A, B and C
= 20000 $\times$ 5 + 15000 $\times$ 7 : 20000 $\times$ 5 + 16000 $\times$ 7 : 20000 $\times$ 5 + 26000 $\times$ 7
= 205000 : 212000 : 282000 = 205 : 212 : 282.
‚ąī A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(69900\times\frac{205}{699}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 20500; B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(69900\times\frac{212}{699}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 21200;
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(69900\times\frac{282}{699}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 28200.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into partnership. A invests 3 times as much as B invests and B invests two-thirds of what C invests. At the end of the year, the profit earned is ‚āĻ 6600. What is the share of B?
questions.solution:
Let C‚Äôs capital = ‚āĻ $x$. Then, B‚Äôs capital = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{2}{3}x\right)$. A‚Äôs capital = ‚āĻ $\left(3\times\frac{2}{3}x\right)$ = ‚āĻ $2x$.
‚ąī Ratio of their capitals = $2x : \frac{2}{3}x : x$ = 6 : 2 : 3.
Hence, B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(6600\times\frac{2}{11}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 1200.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partnership with capitals in the ratio $\frac{7}{2} : \frac{4}{3} : \frac{6}{5}$. After 4 months A increases his share of capital by 50%. If at the end of the year the total profit earned is ‚āĻ 2430, find the share of each in the profit.
questions.solution:
Ratio of capitals = $\frac{7}{2} : \frac{4}{3} : \frac{6}{5} = \left(\frac{7}{2}\times30\right) : \left(\frac{4}{3}\times30\right) : \left(\frac{6}{5}\times30\right) = 105 : 40 : 36$.
Let the initial capitals of A, B and C be ‚āĻ 105$x$, ‚āĻ 40$x$ and ‚āĻ 36$x$ respectively.
Then, ratio of profits = [105$x$ $\times$ 4 + (150% of 105$x$) $\times$ 8] : (40$x$ $\times$ 12) : (36$x$ $\times$ 12)
= 1680 : 480 : 432 = 35 : 10 : 9.
‚ąī A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(2430\times\frac{35}{54}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 1575; B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(2430\times\frac{10}{54}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 450;
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(2430\times\frac{9}{54}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 405.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into partnership with capitals of ‚āĻ 25000, ‚āĻ 30000 and ‚āĻ 15000 respectively. A is the working partner and he gets 30% of the profit for managing the business. The balance profit is distributed in proportion to the capital investment. At the year-end, A gets ‚āĻ 200 more than B and C together. Find the total profit and the share of each.
questions.solution:
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $x$.
Amount obtained by A for managing = ‚āĻ $\left(x\times\frac{30}{100}\right)$ = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3x}{10}\right)$.
Balance profit = ‚āĻ $\left(x - \frac{3x}{10}\right)$ = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{7x}{10}\right)$.
Ratio of capitals = 25000 : 30000 : 15000 = 5 : 6 : 3.
‚ąī A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left[\left(\frac{7x}{10}\times\frac{5}{14}\right) + \frac{3x}{10}\right]$ = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{11x}{20}\right)$; B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{7x}{10}\times\frac{6}{14}\right)$ = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3x}{10}\right)$;
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{7x}{10}\times\frac{3}{14}\right)$ = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3x}{20}\right)$.
$\frac{3x}{10} + \frac{3x}{20} + 200 = \frac{11x}{20} \Rightarrow x = 2000$.
So, total profit = ‚āĻ 2000.
‚ąī A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{11\times2000}{20}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 1100; B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3\times2000}{10}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 600;
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3\times2000}{20}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 300.

questions.question_text: Four milkmen rented a pasture. A grazed 24 cows for 3 months; B 10 cows for 5 months; C 35 cows for 4 months and D 21 cows for 3 months. If A‚Äôs share of rent is ‚āĻ 720, find the total rent of the field.
questions.solution:
Ratio of shares of A, B, C, D = (24 $\times$ 3) : (10 $\times$ 5) : (35 $\times$ 4) : (21 $\times$ 3)
= 72 : 50 : 140 : 63.
Let total rent be ‚āĻ $x$. Then, A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{72x}{325}\right)$.
‚ąī $\frac{72x}{325} = 720 \Leftrightarrow x = \frac{720\times325}{72} = 3250$.
Hence, total rent of the field is ‚āĻ 3250.

questions.question_text: Two persons A and B take a field on rent. A put on it 21 horses for 3 months and 15 cows for 2 months; B puts 15 cows for 6 months and 40 sheep for $1\frac{1}{2}$ months. If, in one day, 3 horses eat as much as 5 cows eat and 6 cows as much as 10 sheep, what part of the rent should A pay?
questions.solution:
6 cows $\equiv$ 10 sheep $\Rightarrow$ 1 cow $\equiv$ $\frac{5}{3}$ sheep.
3 horses $\equiv$ 5 cows $\Rightarrow$ 1 horse $\equiv$ $\frac{5}{3}$ cows $\equiv$ $\left(\frac{5}{3}\times\frac{5}{3}\right)$ sheep $\equiv$ $\frac{25}{9}$ sheep.
Ratio of shares of A and B
= $\left[\left(21\times\frac{25}{9}\times3\right) + \left(15\times\frac{5}{3}\times2\right)\right] : \left[\left(15\times\frac{5}{3}\times6\right) + \left(40\times\frac{3}{2}\right)\right]$
= 225 : 450 = 1 : 2.
Hence, part of the rent paid by A = $\frac{1}{3}$.

questions.question_text: A, B and C took a house on rent for one year for ‚āĻ 13824. They remained together for 4 months and then C left the house. After 5 more months, B also left the house. How much rent should each pay?
questions.solution:
Monthly rent = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{13824}{12}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 1152.
Rent for first 4 months = ‚āĻ (1152 $\times$ 4) = ‚āĻ 4608.
It is to be divided equally among A, B and C.
‚ąī Share of each = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{4608}{3}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 1536.
Rent for next 5 months = ‚āĻ (1152 $\times$ 5) = ‚āĻ 5760.
It is to be divided equally between A and B.
‚ąī Share of each = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{5760}{2}\right)$ = ‚āĻ 2880.
Rent for last 3 months = ‚āĻ (1152 $\times$ 3) = ‚āĻ 3456.
It is to be paid by A only.
‚ąī Total rent paid by A = ‚āĻ (1536 + 2880 + 3456) = ‚āĻ 7872.
Total rent paid by B = ‚āĻ (1536 + 2880) = ‚āĻ 4416.
Total rent paid by C = ‚āĻ 1536.

questions.question_text: A invested ‚āĻ 76000 in a business. After few months, B joined him with ‚āĻ 57000. At the end of the year, the total profit was divided between them in the ratio 2 : 1. After how many months did B join?
questions.solution:
Suppose B joined after $x$ months.
Then, B‚Äôs money was invested for (12 ‚Äď $x$) months.
‚ąī $\frac{76000\times12}{57000(12-x)} = \frac{2}{1}$
$\Leftrightarrow$ 912000 = 114000 (12 ‚Äď $x$)
$\Leftrightarrow$ 114 (12 ‚Äď $x$) = 912
$\Leftrightarrow$ (12 ‚Äď $x$) = 8 $\Leftrightarrow$ $x$ = 4.
Hence, B joined after 4 months.

questions.question_text: The ratio of investments of two partners A and B is 11 : 12 and the ratio of their profits is 2 : 3. If A invested the money for 8 months, then for how much time B invested his money?
questions.solution:
Suppose A invested ‚āĻ 11$x$ for 8 months and B invested ‚āĻ 12$x$ for $y$ months.
Then, $\frac{11x\times8}{12x\times y} = \frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow 24y = 264 \Rightarrow y = 11$.
Hence, B invested the money for 11 months.

questions.question_text: A, B and C are partners in a business. A, whose money has been used for 4 months, claims $\frac{1}{8}$ of the profit. B, whose money has been used for 6 months, claims $\frac{1}{3}$ of the profit. C had invested ‚āĻ 1560 for 8 months. How much money did A and B contribute?
questions.solution:
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, A‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\frac{x}{8}$; B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\frac{x}{3}$;
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left[x - \left(\frac{x}{8} + \frac{x}{3}\right)\right]$ = ‚āĻ $\left(x - \frac{11x}{24}\right)$ = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{13x}{24}\right)$.
‚ąī Ratio of shares of A, B and C = $\frac{1}{8} : \frac{1}{3} : \frac{13}{24}$ = 3 : 8 : 13.
Suppose A invested ‚āĻ $y$ for 4 months and B invested ‚āĻ $z$ for 6 months.
Then, $\frac{y\times4}{1560\times8} = \frac{3}{13} \Rightarrow 52y = 37440 \Rightarrow y = 720$.
And, $\frac{z\times6}{1560\times8} = \frac{8}{13} \Rightarrow 78z = 99840 \Rightarrow z = 1280$.
Hence, A‚Äôs contribution = ‚āĻ 720; B‚Äôs contribution = ‚āĻ 1280.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partnership by investing in the ratio of 3 : 2 : 4. After one year, B invests another ‚āĻ 270000 and C, at the end of 2 years, also invests ‚āĻ 270000. At the end of three years, profits are shared in the ratio of 3 : 4 : 5. Find the initial investment of each.
questions.solution:
Let the initial investments of A, B and C be ‚āĻ 3$x$, ‚āĻ 2$x$ and ‚āĻ 4$x$ respectively.
Then, (3$x$ $\times$ 36) : [(2$x$ $\times$ 12) + (2$x$ + 270000) $\times$ 24] : [(4$x$ $\times$ 24) + (4$x$ + 270000) $\times$ 12] = 3 : 4 : 5
$\Leftrightarrow$ 108$x$ : (72$x$ + 6480000) : (144$x$ + 3240000) = 3 : 4 : 5
‚ąī $\frac{108x}{72x + 6480000} = \frac{3}{4}$
$\Leftrightarrow$ 432$x$ = 216$x$ + 19440000 $\Leftrightarrow$ 216$x$ = 19440000 $\Leftrightarrow$ $x$ = 90000.
Hence, A‚Äôs initial investment = 3$x$ = ‚āĻ 270000;
B‚Äôs initial investment = 2$x$ = ‚āĻ 180000;
C‚Äôs initial investment = 4$x$ = ‚āĻ 360000.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partnership. Their capital contribution is in the ratio 21 : 18 : 14. At the end of the business term they share profits in the ratio 15 : 8 : 9. Find the ratio of time for which they invest their capitals.
questions.solution:
Let the ratio of time be $t_1 : t_2 : t_3$.
Then, (21 $\times$ $t_1$) : (18 $\times$ $t_2$) : (14 $\times$ $t_3$) = 15 : 8 : 9.
$t_1 = \frac{15}{21} = \frac{5}{7}$; $t_2 = \frac{8}{18} = \frac{4}{9}$; $t_3 = \frac{9}{14}$.
Ratio = $\frac{5}{7} : \frac{4}{9} : \frac{9}{14} = \left(\frac{5}{7}\times126\right) : \left(\frac{4}{9}\times126\right) : \left(\frac{9}{14}\times126\right) = 90 : 56 : 81$.

questions.question_text: Rahul, Arun and Sumit started a business. Rahul invested $\frac{1}{2}$ part, Arun $\frac{1}{3}$ part and rest of the capital was invested by Sumit. The ratio of their profits will be
questions.solution:
Let the total capital be ‚āĻ $x$. Then, Rahul‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{2}$,
Arun‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{3}$.
Sumit‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left[x - \left(\frac{x}{2} + \frac{x}{3}\right)\right] = \frac{x}{6}$.
Required ratio = $\frac{x}{2} : \frac{x}{3} : \frac{x}{6} = 3 : 2 : 1$.

questions.question_text: P and Q started a business investing ‚āĻ 85000 and ‚āĻ 15000 respectively. In what ratio the profit earned after 2 years be divided between P and Q respectively?
Anand and Deepak started a business investing ‚āĻ 22500 and ‚āĻ 35000 respectively. Out of a total profit of ‚āĻ 13800, Deepak‚Äôs share is
questions.solution: Ratio of their shares = 22500 : 35000 = 9 : 14. Deepak‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(13800 \times \frac{14}{23}\right) = ‚āĻ 8400$.

questions.question_text: Samaira, Mahira and Kiara rented a set of DVDs at a rent of ‚āĻ 578. If they used it for 8 hours, 12 hours and 14 hours respectively, what is Kiara‚Äôs share of rent to be paid?
questions.solution:
Ratio of shares = 8 : 12 : 14 = 4 : 6 : 7.
Kiara‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(578 \times \frac{7}{17}\right) = ‚āĻ 238$.

questions.question_text: P, Q and R invested ‚āĻ 45000, ‚āĻ 70000 and ‚āĻ 90000 respectively to start a business. At the end of 2 years, they earned a profit of ‚āĻ 164000. What will be Q‚Äôs share in the profit?

Prakash, Sachin and Anil started a business jointly investing ‚āĻ 11 lakh, ‚āĻ 16.5 lakh and ‚āĻ 8.25 lakh respectively. The profit earned by them in the business at the end of 3 years was ‚āĻ 19.5 lakh. What will be 50% of Anil‚Äôs share in the profit?
questions.solution:
Ratio of shares = 11 : 16.5 : 8.25 = 4 : 6 : 3.
Anil‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(19.5 \times \frac{3}{13}\right)$ lakh = ‚āĻ 4.5 lakh.
Required amount = 50% of ‚āĻ 4.5 lakh = ‚āĻ 2.25 lakh.

questions.question_text: Two friends invested ‚āĻ 1500 and ‚āĻ 2500 in a business. They earned a profit of ‚āĻ 800. One-half of the profit was divided equally between them and the other half was divided in proportion to their capitals. How much did each of them receive?
questions.solution:
Ratio of shares = 1500 : 2500 = 3 : 5.
Share of first friend = ‚āĻ $\left[400 + \left(400 \times \frac{3}{8}\right)\right] = ‚āĻ (200 + 150) = ‚āĻ 350$.
Share of second friend = ‚āĻ $\left[400 + \left(400 \times \frac{5}{8}\right)\right] = ‚āĻ (200 + 250) = ‚āĻ 450$.

questions.question_text: Three persons stared a placement business with a capital of ‚āĻ 3000. B invests ‚āĻ 600 less than A and C invests ‚āĻ 300 less than B. What is B‚Äôs share in a profit of ‚āĻ 886?
questions.solution:
Let A‚Äôs capital = ‚āĻ $x$. Then, B‚Äôs capital = ‚āĻ $(x - 600)$.
C‚Äôs capital = ‚āĻ $[(x - 600) - 300] = ‚āĻ (x - 900)$.
$x + (x - 600) + (x - 900) = 3000$
$\Rightarrow 3x = 4500 \Rightarrow x = 1500$.
So, A : B : C = 1500 : 900 : 600 = 5 : 3 : 2.
Hence, B‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(886 \times \frac{3}{10}\right) = ‚āĻ 265.80$.

questions.question_text: Reena and Shaloo are partners in a business. Reena invests ‚āĻ 35000 for 8 months and Shaloo invests ‚āĻ 42000 for 10 months. Out of a profit of ‚āĻ 31570, Reena‚Äôs share is :
questions.solution:
Ratio of their shares = $(35000 \times 8) : (42000 \times 10) = 2 : 3$.
Reena‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(31570 \times \frac{2}{5}\right) = ‚āĻ 12628$.

questions.question_text: Shankar started a business with an investment of ‚āĻ 120,000. After three months, Aniket joined him with an investment of ‚āĻ 190,000. They earned a profit of ‚āĻ 17,50,000 after one year. What is Aniket‚Äôs share in the profit?
questions.solution:
Shankar : Aniket = $(120000 \times 12) : (190000 \times 9) = 1440000 : 1710000 = 16 : 19$.
Aniket‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(1750000 \times \frac{19}{35}\right) = ‚āĻ 950000$.

questions.question_text: Arun started a business investing ‚āĻ 38000. After 5 months Bakul joined him with a capital of ‚āĻ 55000. At the end of the year the total profit was ‚āĻ 22000. What is the approximate difference between the shares of profits of Arun and Bakul?
questions.solution:
Arun : Bakul = $(38000 \times 12) : (55000 \times 7) = 456000 : 385000 = 456 : 385$.
Required difference = ‚āĻ $\left[22000 \times \left(\frac{456}{841} - \frac{385}{841}\right)\right] = ‚āĻ \left(22000 \times \frac{71}{841}\right) = ‚āĻ 1857.31 \approx ‚āĻ 1857$.

questions.question_text: Gautam started a business with a sum of ‚āĻ 60000. Jatin joined him 8 months later with a sum of ‚āĻ 35000. At what respective ratio will the two share the profit after two years?

Simran started a software business by investing ‚āĻ 50000. After six months, Nanda joined her with a capital of ‚āĻ 80000. After 3 years, they earned a profit of ‚āĻ 24500. What was Simran‚Äôs share in the profit?
questions.solution:
Simran : Nanda = $(50000 \times 36) : (80000 \times 30) = 1800000 : 2400000 = 3 : 4$.
Simran‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(24500 \times \frac{3}{7}\right) = ‚āĻ 10500$.

questions.question_text: Dilip, Ram and Avtar started a shop by investing ‚āĻ 2700, ‚āĻ 8100 and ‚āĻ 7200 respectively. At the end of one year, the profit earned was distributed. If Ram‚Äôs share was ‚āĻ 3600, what was their total profit?
questions.solution:
Dilip : Ram : Avtar = 2700 : 8100 : 7200 = 3 : 9 : 8.
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, Ram‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{9}{20}x\right)$.
$\frac{9}{20}x = 3600 \Rightarrow x = \left(\frac{3600 \times 20}{9}\right) = 8000$.
Hence, total profit = ‚āĻ 8000.

questions.question_text: A and B started a business in partnership investing ‚āĻ 20000 and ‚āĻ 15000 respectively. After six months, C joined them with ‚āĻ 20000. What will be B‚Äôs share in the total profit of ‚āĻ 25000 earned at the end of 2 years from the starting of the business?

Aman started a business investing ‚āĻ 70000. Rakhi joined him after six months with an amount of ‚āĻ 105000 and Sagar joined them with ‚āĻ 1.4 lakhs after another six months. The amount of profit earned should be distributed in what ratio among Aman, Rakhi and Sagar respectively, 3 years after Aman started the business?

Sonia started a business investing ‚āĻ 60000. After 6 months Vivek joined him with an amount of ‚āĻ 140000. After 1 year Kirti also joined them with ‚āĻ 120000. After 2 years the business yielded a total profit of ‚āĻ 450000. What is the share of Vivek in the profit?

Arun, Kamal and Vinay invested ‚āĻ 8000, ‚āĻ 4000 and ‚āĻ 8000 respectively in a business. Arun left after six months. If after eight months, there was a gain of ‚āĻ 4005, then what will be the share of Kamal?
questions.solution:
Arun : Kamal : Vinay = $(8000 \times 6) : (4000 \times 8) : (8000 \times 8) = 48 : 32 : 64 = 3 : 2 : 4$.
Kamal‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(4005 \times \frac{2}{9}\right) = ‚āĻ 890$.

questions.question_text: P and Q started a business in the ratio of 2 : 3. After 1 year P left the business but Q continues. After 2 years he had the profit of ‚āĻ 26000. What is the profit of Q?
questions.solution:
Let the initial capitals of P and Q be ‚āĻ $2x$ and ‚āĻ $3x$ respectively.
Then, Ratio of profits = $(2x \times 12) : (3x \times 24) = 24x : 72x = 1 : 3$.
Q‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(26000 \times \frac{3}{4}\right) = ‚āĻ 19500$.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partnership. A invests some money at the beginning, B invests double the amount after six months and C invests thrice the amount after eight months. If the annual profit be ‚āĻ 27000; C‚Äôs share (in ‚āĻ) is
questions.solution:
Let the money invested by A, B and C be ‚āĻ $x$, ‚āĻ $2x$ and ‚āĻ $3x$ respectively.
Then, A : B : C = $(x \times 12) : (2x \times 6) : (3x \times 4) = 12x : 12x : 12x = 1 : 1 : 1$.
C‚Äôs share = ‚āĻ $\left(27000 \times \frac{1}{3}\right) = ‚āĻ 9000$.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partner ship. They invest ‚āĻ 40000, ‚āĻ 80000 and ‚āĻ 120000 respectively. At the end of the first year, B withdraws ‚āĻ 40,000, while at the end of the second year, C withdraws ‚āĻ 80000. In what ratio will the profit be shared at the end of 3 years?

A, B and C enter into a partnership. A initially invests ‚āĻ 25 lakhs and adds another ‚āĻ 10 lakhs after one year. B initially invests ‚āĻ 35 lakhs and withdraws ‚āĻ 10 lakhs after 2 years and C invests ‚āĻ 30 lakhs. In what ratio should the profits be divided at the end of 3 years?
questions.solution:
$A:B:C=(25\text{ lakhs}	imes1+35\text{ lakhs}	imes2):(35\text{ lakhs}	imes2+25\text{ lakhs}	imes1):(30\text{ lakhs}	imes3)$
$=95\text{ lakhs}:95\text{ lakhs}:90\text{ lakhs}=19:19:18.$

questions.question_text: Subhash starts a business by investing ‚āĻ 25000. 6 months later Aditya joins him by investing ‚āĻ 15000. After another 6 months Aditya invests an additional amount of ‚āĻ 15000. At the end of 3 years they earn a profit of ‚āĻ 247000. What is Aditya‚Äôs share in the profit?
questions.solution:
Subhash : Aditya = $(25000	imes36):(15000	imes6+30000	imes24)$
$=900000:810000=10:9.$
Aditya's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{9}{19}\times247000\right)$ = ‚āĻ 117000.

questions.question_text: Shekhar started a business investing ‚āĻ 25000 in 2009. In 2010, he invested an additional amount of ‚āĻ 10000 and Rajeev joined him with an amount of ‚āĻ 35000. In 2011, Shekhar invested another additional amount of ‚āĻ 10000 and Jatin joined them with an amount of ‚āĻ 35000. What will be Rajeev‚Äôs share in the profit of ‚āĻ 150000 earned at the end of 3 years from the start of the business in 2009?

A and B entered into a partnership investing ‚āĻ 16000 and ‚āĻ 12000 respectively. After 3 months, A withdrew ‚āĻ 5000 while B invested ‚āĻ 5000 more. After 3 more months, C joins the business with a capital of ‚āĻ 21000. The share of B exceeds that of C, out of a total profit of ‚āĻ 26,400 after one year by
questions.solution:
$A:B:C=(16000	imes3+11000	imes9):(12000	imes3+17000	imes9):(21000	imes6)$
$=147:189:126=7:9:6.$
Difference between B's and C's shares
= ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{9}{22}\times26400-\frac{6}{22}\times26400\right)$ = ‚āĻ 3600.

questions.question_text: A and B start a business with investments of ‚āĻ 5000 and ‚āĻ 4500 respectively. After 4 months, A takes out half of his capital. After two more months, B takes out one-third of his capital while C joins them with a capital of ‚āĻ 7000. At the end of a year, they earn a profit of ‚āĻ 5080. Find the share of each member in the profit.
A, B, C subscribe ‚āĻ 50000 for a business. A subscribes ‚āĻ 4000 more than B and B ‚āĻ 5000 more than C. Out of a total profit of ‚āĻ 35000, A receives:

A, B and C are three partners. They altogether invested ‚āĻ 14000 in business. At the end of the year, A got ‚āĻ 337.50, B ‚āĻ 1125 and C ‚āĻ 637.50 as profit. The difference between the investments of B and A was
questions.solution:
Ratio of investments of A, B and C = Ratio of their profits
$=337.50:1125:637.50=9:30:17.$
A's investment = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{9}{56}\times14000\right)$ = ‚āĻ 2250.
B's investment = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{30}{56}\times14000\right)$ = ‚āĻ 7500.
Hence, required difference = ‚āĻ $(7500-2250)$ = ‚āĻ 5250.

questions.question_text: A, B and C started a business investing amounts in the ratio of 5 : 6 : 8 respectively. After one year, C withdrew 50% of the amount and A invested an additional amount of 60% of the original amount invested by him. In what ratio, the profit earned at the end of 2 years should be distributed among A, B and C respectively?
questions.solution:
Let the initial investments of A, B and C be $5x, 6x$ and $8x$ respectively. Then,
$A:B:C=[5x\times12+(160\%\text{ of }5x)\times12]:(6x\times24):(8x\times12+4x\times12)$
$=156x:144x:144x=13:12:12.$

questions.question_text: John, Mona and Gordon, three US based business partners, jointly invested in a business project to supply nuclear fuel to India. As per their share in the investment, Gordon will receive $\frac{2}{3}$ of the profits whereas John and Mona divide the remainder equally. It is estimated that the income of John will increase by $ 60 million when the rate of profit rises from 4% to 7%. What is Mona‚Äôs capital ?
questions.solution:
Fraction of profit received by each one of John and Mona
$=\left(1-\frac{2}{3}\right)\times\frac{1}{2}=\frac{1}{6}.$
Ratio of capitals of John, Mona and Gordon = Ratio of their profits
$=1:1:4.$
Let the total capital be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, $\frac{1}{6}$ of $(7\%\text{ of }x-4\%\text{ of }x) = \$60\text{ million}$
$\Rightarrow3\%\text{ of }x = \$360\text{ million}$
$\Rightarrow x = \$360\times\frac{100}{3}\text{ million} = \$12000\text{ million}.$
Mona's capital = $\left(\frac{1}{6}\times\$12000\text{ million}\right) = \$2000\text{ million}.$

questions.question_text: Anu is a working partner and Bimla is a sleeping partner in a business. Anu puts in ‚āĻ 5000 and Bimla puts in ‚āĻ 6000. Anu receives 12.5% of the profit for managing the business and the rest is divided in proportion to their capitals. What does each get out of a profit of ‚āĻ 880?
questions.solution:
Anu : Bimla = $5000:6000=5:6.$
Anu's share for managing business = $12.5\%\text{ of }‚āĻ880=‚āĻ110.$
Net profit = ‚āĻ $(880-110)=‚āĻ770.$
Anu's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{5}{11}\times770\right)=‚āĻ350.$
Anu's total share = ‚āĻ $(110+350)=‚āĻ460.$
Bimla's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{6}{11}\times770\right)=‚āĻ420.$

questions.question_text: Two partners invested ‚āĻ 125000 and ‚āĻ 85000 respectively in a business. They distribute 60% of the profit equally and decide to distribute the remaining 40% as the interest on their capitals. If one partner received ‚āĻ 3000 more than the other, the total profit is
questions.solution:
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, $60\%\text{ of the profit} = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{60}{100}\times x\right) = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{3}{5}x\right).$
From this part of the profit each gets = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3}{10}x\right).$
$40\%\text{ of total profit} = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{40}{100}\times x\right) = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{2}{5}x\right).$
Now, this amount of ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{2}{5}x\right)$ has been divided in the ratio of capitals, which is $125000:85000$ or $25:17$ as interests.
Interest on first capital = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{2x}{5}\times\frac{25}{42}\right) = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{5x}{21}\right).$
Interest on second capital = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{2x}{5}\times\frac{17}{42}\right) = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{17x}{105}\right).$
Total money received by first partner = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3x}{10}+\frac{5x}{21}\right) = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{113x}{210}\right).$
Total money received by second partner = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{3x}{10}+\frac{17x}{105}\right) = ‚āĻ \left(\frac{97x}{210}\right).$
$\frac{113x}{210}-\frac{97x}{210}=3000\text{ or }x=39375.$
Hence, total profit = ‚āĻ 39375.

questions.question_text: Three partners A, B, C start a business. Twice A‚Äôs capital is equal to thrice B‚Äôs capital and B‚Äôs capital is four times C‚Äôs capital. Out of a total profit of ‚āĻ 16500 at the end of the year, B‚Äôs share is :
questions.solution:
Let $C=x$. Then, $B=4x$ and $2A=3\times4x=12x\text{ or }A=6x.$
$A:B:C=6x:4x:x=6:4:1.$
So, B's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{4}{11}\times16500\right)=‚āĻ6000.$

questions.question_text: If 4 (A‚Äôs capital) = 6 (B‚Äôs capital) = 10 (C‚Äôs capital), then out of a profit of ‚āĻ 4650, C will receive
questions.solution:
Let $4A=6B=10C=k.$
Then, $A=\frac{k}{4}, B=\frac{k}{6}\text{ and }C=\frac{k}{10}.$
$A:B:C=\frac{k}{4}:\frac{k}{6}:\frac{k}{10}=15:10:6.$
Hence, C's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{6}{31}\times4650\right)=‚āĻ900.$

questions.question_text: Ninad, Vikas and Manav enter into a partnership. Ninad invests some amount at the beginning. Vikas invests double the amount after 6 months and Manav invests thrice the amount invested by Ninad after 8 months. They earn a profit of ‚āĻ 45000 at the end of the year. What is Manav‚Äôs share in the profit?
questions.solution:
Let Ninad's investment be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, Ratio of capitals = $(x\times12):(2x\times6):(3x\times4)=12x:12x:12x=1:1:1.$
Manav's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{1}{3}\times45000\right)=‚āĻ15000.$

questions.question_text: Four milkmen rented a pasture. A grazed 15 cows for 4 months, B grazed 12 cows for 2 months, C grazed 18 cows for 6 months and D grazed 16 cows for 5 months. If A‚Äôs share of rent is ‚āĻ 1020, what is C‚Äôs share of rent?
questions.solution:
$A:B:C:D=15\times4:12\times2:18\times6:16\times5=60:24:108:80=15:6:27:20.$
Let the total rent be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, A's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{15}{68}x\right).$
$\frac{15}{68}x=1020\Rightarrow x=\left(1020\times\frac{68}{15}\right)=4624.$
Hence, C's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{27}{68}\times4624\right)=‚āĻ1836.$

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partnership by making investments in the ratio 3 : 5 : 7. After a year, C invests another ‚āĻ 337600 while A withdraws ‚āĻ 45600. The ratio of investments then changes to 24 : 59 : 16.7. How much did A invest initially?
questions.solution:
Let the initial investments of A, B, C, be ‚āĻ $3x$, ‚āĻ $5x$ and ‚āĻ $7x$ respectively. Then,
$(3x-45600):5x:(7x+337600)=24:59:167.$
$\frac{3x-45600}{5x}=\frac{24}{59}\Rightarrow177x-2690400=120x\Rightarrow57x=2690400\Rightarrow x=47200.$
Hence, A's initial investment = ‚āĻ $(47200\times3)=‚āĻ141600.$

questions.question_text: A, B and C are partners in a business. Their shares are in the proportion of $\frac{1}{3} : \frac{1}{4} : \frac{1}{5}$. A withdraws half of his capital after 15 months and after another 15 months, a profit of ‚āĻ 4340 is divided. The share of C is
questions.solution:
Ratio of initial investments = $\frac{1}{3}:\frac{1}{4}:\frac{1}{5}=20:15:12.$
Let their initial investments be $20x, 15x$ and $12x$ respectively.
$A:B:C=(20x\times15+10x\times15):(15x\times30):(12x\times30)=450x:450x:360x=5:5:4.$
C's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{4}{14}\times4340\right)=‚āĻ1240.$

questions.question_text: A, B, C started a business with their investments in the ratio 1 : 3 : 5. After 4 months, A invested the same amount as before and B as well as C withdrew half of their investments. The ratio of their profits at the end of the year is :
questions.solution:
Let their initial investments be $x, 3x$ and $5x$ respectively. Then,
$A:B:C=(x\times4+2x\times8):(3x\times4+\frac{3x}{2}\times8):(5x\times4+\frac{5x}{2}\times8)=20x:24x:40x=5:6:10.$

questions.question_text: A and B entered into partnership with capitals in the ratio 4 : 5. After 3 months, A withdrew $\frac{1}{4}$ of his capital and B withdrew $\frac{1}{5}$ of his capital. The gain at the end of 10 months was ‚āĻ 760. A‚Äôs share in this profit is :
questions.solution:
$A:B=\left[4x\times3+\left(4x-\frac{1}{4}x\right)\times7\right]:\left[5x\times3+\left(5x-\frac{1}{5}x\right)\times7\right]$
$=(12x+21x):(15x+28x)=33:43.$
A's share = ‚āĻ $\left(\frac{33}{76}\times760\right)=‚āĻ330.$

questions.question_text: In a partnership, A invests $\frac{1}{6}$ of the capital for $\frac{1}{6}$ of the time, B invests $\frac{1}{3}$ of the capital for $\frac{1}{3}$ of the time and C, the rest of the capital for the whole time. Out of a profit of ‚āĻ 4600, B‚Äôs share is
questions.solution:
Suppose A invests ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{6}$ for $y$ months.
Then, B invests ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{3}$ for $ rac{y}{3}$ months.
C invests $x -  rac{x}{6} -  rac{x}{3} =  rac{x}{2}$ for $y$ months.
$	herefore$ A : B : C = $ rac{x}{6} 	imes y :  rac{x}{3} 	imes  rac{y}{3} :  rac{x}{2} 	imes y$
$=  rac{1}{6} :  rac{1}{9} :  rac{1}{2} = 3 : 2 : 9$
Hence, B's share = ‚āĻ $ rac{4}{23} 	imes 4600$ = ‚āĻ 800.

questions.question_text: A, B and C jointly thought of engaging themselves in a business venture. It was agreed that A would invest ‚āĻ 6500 for 6 months, B, ‚āĻ 8400 for 5 months and C, ‚āĻ 10,000 for 3 months. A wants to be the working member for which he was to receive 5% of the profits. The profit earned was ‚āĻ 7400. Calculate the share of B in the profit.
questions.solution:
For managing, A receives = 5% of ‚āĻ 7400 = ‚āĻ 370.
Balance = ‚āĻ (7400 - 370) = ‚āĻ 7030.
Ratio of their investments = (6500 $	imes$ 6) : (8400 $	imes$ 5) : (10000 $	imes$ 3)
= 39000 : 42000 : 30000 = 13 : 14 : 10.
$	herefore$ B's share = ‚āĻ $ rac{14}{37} 	imes 7030$ = ‚āĻ 2660.

questions.question_text: Manick received ‚āĻ 6000 as his share out of the total profit of ‚āĻ 9000 which he and Raunaq earned at the end of one year. If Manick invested ‚āĻ 20000 for 6 months, whereas Raunaq invested his amount for the whole year, what was the amount invested by Raunaq?
questions.solution:
Suppose Raunaq invested ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, Manick : Raunaq = (20000 $	imes$ 6) : ($x$ $	imes$ 12)
$ rac{120000}{12x} =  rac{6000}{3000} 	imes  rac{1}{24}$ or $x =  rac{120000}{24} = 5000$.

questions.question_text: A, B and C entered into a partnership. A invested ‚āĻ 2560 and B invested ‚āĻ 2000. At the end of the year, they gained ‚āĻ 1105, out of which A got ‚āĻ 320. C‚Äôs capital was
questions.solution:
Let C's capital be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, A : B : C = 2560 : 2000 : $x$.
A's share = ‚āĻ $ rac{2560}{4560 + x} 	imes 1105$
$1105 	imes  rac{2560}{4560 + x} = 320$
$	herefore$ 320$x$ + 1459200 = 2828800
$	herefore$ 320$x$ = 1369600
$	herefore$ $x$ = 4280.

questions.question_text: A, B and C enter into a partnership. A contributes one-third of the capital while B contributes as much as A and C together contribute. If the profit at the end of the year amounts to ‚āĻ 900, what would C receive?
questions.solution:
Let total capital = ‚āĻ $x$. Then, A's capital = ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{3}$.
B's capital = (A + C)'s capital $	herefore$ 2(B's capital) = (A + B + C)'s capital = ‚āĻ $x$
$	herefore$ B's capital = ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{2}$.
C's capital = ‚āĻ $ rac{x}{6}$.
$	herefore$ A : B : C = $ rac{x}{3} :  rac{x}{2} :  rac{x}{6} = 2 : 3 : 1$.
So, C's share = ‚āĻ $ rac{1}{6} 	imes 900$ = ‚āĻ 150.

questions.question_text: A and B started a business jointly. A‚Äôs investment was thrice the investment of B and the period of his investment was two times the period of investment of B. If B received ‚āĻ 4000 as profit, then their total profit is:
questions.solution:
Suppose B invested ‚āĻ $x$ for $y$ months.
Then, A invested ‚āĻ 3$x$ for 2$y$ months.
So, A : B = (3$x$ $	imes$ 2$y$) : ($x$ $	imes$ $y$) = 6$xy$ : $xy$ = 6 : 1.
$	herefore$ B's profit : Total profit = 1 : 7.
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, $ rac{1}{7} =  rac{4000}{x}$ or $x$ = 28000.

questions.question_text: A started a business with ‚āĻ 21000 and is joined afterwards by B with ‚āĻ 36000. After how many months did B join if the profits at the end of the year are divided equally?
questions.solution:
Suppose B joined after $x$ months.
Then, 21000 $	imes$ 12 = 36000 $	imes$ (12 - $x$)
$	herefore$ 36$x$ = 180 $	herefore$ $x$ = 5.
Hence, B joined after 5 months.

questions.question_text: A began a business with ‚āĻ 85000. He was joined afterwards by B with ‚āĻ 42500. For how much period does B join, if the profits at the end of the year are divided in the ratio of 3 : 1?
questions.solution:
Suppose B joined for $x$ months.
Then, $ rac{85000 	imes 12}{42500 	imes x} =  rac{3}{1} 	herefore x = 8$.

questions.question_text: A starts business with ‚āĻ 3500 and after 5 months, B joins with A as his partner. After a year, the profit is divided in the ratio 2 : 3. What is B‚Äôs contribution in the capital?
questions.solution:
Let B's capital be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, $ rac{3500 	imes 12}{7x} =  rac{2}{3} 	herefore 14x = 126000 	herefore x = 9000$.

questions.question_text: A and B enter into a partnership with ‚āĻ 50000 and ‚āĻ 60000 respectively. C joins them after x months, contributing ‚āĻ 70000 and B leaves x months before the end of the year. If they share the profit in the ratio of 20 : 18 : 21, then the value of x is
questions.solution:
Clearly, A invested his capital for 12 months while each one of B and C invested his capital for (12 - $x$) months.
Ratio of profits of A, B and C
= (50000 $	imes$ 12) : [60000 $	imes$ (12 - $x$)] : [70000 $	imes$ (12 - $x$)]
= 60 : 6(12 - $x$) : 7(12 - $x$)
= 60 : (72 - 6$x$) : (84 - 7$x$)
But ratio of profits = 20 : 18 : 21 = 60 : 54 : 63.
$	herefore$ 60 : (72 - 6$x$) : (84 - 7$x$) = 60 : 54 : 63
So, 72 - 6$x$ = 54 $	herefore$ 6$x$ = 18 $	herefore$ $x$ = 3.

questions.question_text: In a business A invests ‚āĻ 600 more than B. The capital of B remained invested for $7\frac{1}{2}$ months, while the capital of A remained invested for 2 more months. If the total profit be ‚āĻ 620 and B gets ‚āĻ 140 less than what A gets, then A‚Äôs capital is

A and B start a business jointly. A invests ‚āĻ 16000 for 8 months and B remains in the business for 4 months. Out of the total profit, B claims $\frac{2}{7}$ of the profit. How much money was contributed by B?
questions.solution:
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $x$.
Then, B = $ rac{2}{7}x$ and A = ($x$ - $ rac{2}{7}x$) = $ rac{5}{7}x$.
So, A : B = $ rac{5}{7} :  rac{2}{7} = 5 : 2$.
Let B's capital be ‚āĻ $y$.
Then, $ rac{16000 	imes 8}{y 	imes 4} =  rac{5}{2} 	herefore y = 12800$.

questions.question_text: Two friends P and Q started a business investing in the ratio of 5 : 6. R joined them after six months investing an amount equal to that of Q‚Äôs. At the end of the year, 20% profit was earned which was equal to ‚āĻ 98,000. What was the amount invested by R?
questions.solution:
Let the total investment be ‚āĻ $z$.
Then, 20% of $z$ = 98000 $	herefore$ $z$ = $ rac{98000 	imes 100}{20} = 490000$.
Let the capitals of P, Q and R be ‚āĻ 5$x$, ‚āĻ 6$x$ and ‚āĻ 6$x$ respectively.
Then, (5$x$ $	imes$ 12) + (6$x$ $	imes$ 12) + (6$x$ $	imes$ 6) = 490000 $	imes$ 12
$	herefore$ 168$x$ = 490000 $	imes$ 12 $	herefore$ $x$ = $ rac{490000 	imes 12}{168} = 35000$.
$	herefore$ R's investment = 6$x$ = ‚āĻ (6 $	imes$ 35000) = ‚āĻ 210000.

questions.question_text: Three partners shared the profit in a business in the ratio 5 : 7 : 8. They had partnered for 14 months, 8 months and 7 months respectively. What was the ratio of their investments?
questions.solution:
Let their investments be ‚āĻ $x$ for 14 months; ‚āĻ $y$ for 8 months and ‚āĻ $z$ for 7 months respectively.
Then, 14$x$ : 8$y$ : 7$z$ = 5 : 7 : 8.
Now, $ rac{14$x$}{8$y$} =  rac{5}{7} 	herefore$ 70$x$ = 40$y 	herefore y =  rac{7}{4}x$.
And, $ rac{14$x$}{7$z$} =  rac{5}{8} 	herefore$ 112$x$ = 35$z 	herefore z =  rac{112}{35}x =  rac{16}{5}x$.
$	herefore$ $x : y : z = x :  rac{7}{4}x :  rac{16}{5}x = 20 : 35 : 64$.

questions.question_text: A, B and C invested their capitals in the ratio 3 : 4 : 6. However their shares of profit are equal. The durations of their investments must be in the ratio
questions.solution:
Let their investments be ‚āĻ 3$x$ for $p$ months; ‚āĻ 4$x$ for $q$ months and ‚āĻ 6$x$ for $r$ months respectively.
Then, 3$xp$ : 4$xq$ : 6$xr$ = 1 : 1 : 1
$	herefore$ 3$p$ : 4$q$ : 6$r$ = 1 : 1 : 1
So, 3$p$ = 4$q 	herefore q =  rac{3}{4}p$. And, 4$q$ = 6$r 	herefore r =  rac{4q}{6} =  rac{4}{6} 	imes  rac{3}{4}p =  rac{1}{2}p$.
$	herefore$ $p : q : r = p :  rac{3}{4}p :  rac{1}{2}p = 4 : 3 : 2$.

questions.question_text: A and B invest in a business in the ratio 3 : 2. If 5% of the total profit goes to charity and A‚Äôs share is ‚āĻ 855, the total profit is
questions.solution:
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ 100.
After paying to charity, A's share = ‚āĻ (95 $	imes$ $ rac{3}{5}$) = ‚āĻ 57.
If A's share is ‚āĻ 57, total profit = ‚āĻ 100.
If A's share is ‚āĻ 855, total profit = $ rac{100 	imes 855}{57} = 1500$.

questions.question_text: Swati and Rajni enter into a partnership with their capitals in the ratio 5 : 6. At the end of 7 months Swati withdraws her capital. If they receive the profit in the ratio of 5 : 9, find how long was Rajni‚Äôs capital used.
questions.solution:
Suppose Swati invested ‚āĻ 5$x$ for 7 months and Rajni invested ‚āĻ 6$x$ for $y$ months. Then,
$ rac{5x 	imes 7}{6x 	imes y} =  rac{5}{9}$
$	herefore$ 30$y$ = 315 $	herefore$ $y$ = 10 $ rac{1}{2}$.
Hence, Rajni's capital was used for 10 $ rac{1}{2}$ months.

questions.question_text: X and Y are partners in a business. X contributed $\frac{1}{3}$ of the capital for 9 months and Y received $\frac{2}{5}$ of the profits. For how long was Y‚Äôs money used in the business?
questions.solution:
Let the total profit be ‚āĻ $z$. Then, Y's share = ‚āĻ $ rac{2}{5}z$, X's share = ‚āĻ ($z$ - $ rac{2}{5}z$) = ‚āĻ $ rac{3}{5}z$.
$	herefore$ X : Y = $ rac{3}{5} :  rac{2}{5} = 3 : 2$.
Let the total capital be ‚āĻ $x$ and suppose Y's money was used for $y$ months. Then,
$ rac{ rac{1}{3}x 	imes 9}{ rac{2}{3}x 	imes y} =  rac{3}{2} 	herefore 18x = 6xy 	herefore y = 3$.
Hence, Y's money was used for 3 months.

questions.question_text: A and B started a business with initial investments in the respective ratio of 18 : 7. After four months from the start of the business, A invest ‚āĻ 2000 more and B invested ‚āĻ 7000 more. At the end of one year, if the profit was distributed among them in the ratio of 2 : 1 respective, what was the total initial investment with which A and B started the business?
questions.solution:
Let the initial investment of A and B is 18$x$ and 7$x$. After four months from the start of business, A invest ‚āĻ 2000 more for each eight months. Then total investment of A
= 18$x$ $	imes$ 4 + (18$x$ + 2000) $	imes$ 8
= 72$x$ + 144$x$ + 16000
= 216$x$ + 16000
After four months, from the start of business, B invest ‚āĻ 7000 more for each eight months.
Total investment by B
= 7$x$ $	imes$ 4 + (7$x$ + 7000) $	imes$ 8
= 28$x$ + 56$x$ + 56000
= 84$x$ + 56000
According to the questions.
$ rac{216x + 16000}{84x + 56000} =  rac{2}{1}$
$	herefore$ 216$x$ + 16000 = 168$x$ + 112000
$	herefore$ 216$x$ - 168$x$ = 112000 - 16000
48$x$ = 96000 $	herefore$ $x$ = 2000.
Total initial investment of A and B
= (18 + 7) $	imes$ 2000 = ‚āĻ 50000.

questions.question_text: Anil, Kamal and Vini Invested ‚āĻ 8000, ‚āĻ 4000 and ‚āĻ 8000 respectively in a business. Anil left after six months. If after eight months, there was a gain of ‚āĻ 4005, then what will be the share of Kamal?
questions.solution:
Ratio of profit of Anil : Kamal : Vini
= (8000 $	imes$ 6) : (4000 $	imes$ 8) : (8000 $	imes$ 8) = 48000 : 32000 : 64000
= 48 : 32 : 64
= 3 : 2 : 4
$	herefore$ Kamal's share = ‚āĻ (4005 $	imes$ $ rac{2}{9}$) = ‚āĻ 890.

questions.question_text: A starts a business by investing ‚āĻ 28,000. After 2 months, B joins with ‚āĻ 20,000 and after another two months C joins with ‚āĻ 18,000. At the end of 10 months from the start of the business, if B withdraws ‚āĻ 2,000 and C withdrawn ‚āĻ 2,000, in what ratio should the profit be distributed among A, B and C at the end of the year?
questions.solution:
A invests money for 12 months
B invests money for 10 months
C invests money for 8 months
Ratio of profit of A to B to C
$=28000	imes12:20000	imes8+18000	imes2:18000	imes6+16000	imes2$
$=28	imes12	imes1000:(160+36)	imes1000:(108+32)	imes1000$
$=28	imes12:160+36:108+32$
$=336:196:140=12:7:5$

questions.exam_reference: United India Insurance Co. Ltd. (UIICL)‚ÄĒAssistant (Online) Exam 2015
questions.question_text: In a business, B invests half the amount invested by A. After 6 months from the start of the business, C joins the business with an amount equal to twice of B‚Äôs investment. After 8 months from the start of the business B withdraws completely from the business. If at the end of the years, C‚Äôs share in the profit was ‚āĻ 2460, what was the total profit received that year?
questions.solution:
Let B's investment be ‚āĻ $x$
‚ąī A's investment = ‚āĻ $2x$
‚ąī C's investment = ‚āĻ $2x$
A invests money for 12 months
B invests money for 8 months
C invests money for 6 months.
Ratio of the equivalent capitals of A, B and C for 1 month
$=2x	imes12:x	imes8:2x	imes6=6:2:3$
Sum of the terms of ratio
$=6+2+3=11$
If the total profit at the end of the year be ‚āĻ $a$, then
Share of c = $ rac{3}{11}a=2460$
‚áí $3a=2460	imes11$
‚áí $a= rac{2460	imes11}{3}$ = ‚āĻ $9020$

questions.question_text: A, B and C entered in to a partnership by investing ‚āĻ 15, 400, ‚āĻ 18,200 and ‚āĻ 12,600 respectively. B left after 6 months. If after 8 months, there was a profit of ‚āĻ 28,790, then what is the share of C in the profit?
questions.solution:
Investment of A for 8 months = ‚āĻ $15400$
Investment of B for 6 months = ‚āĻ $18200$
Investment of C for 8 months = ‚āĻ $12600$
Ratio of the share of A, B and C
$=15400	imes8:18200	imes6:12600	imes8$
$=154	imes8:182	imes6:126	imes8$
$=44:39:36$
Sum of the terms of ratio $=44+39+36=119$
Share of C = ‚āĻ $ rac{36}{119}	imes28790$ = ‚āĻ $8710$ ‚Čą ‚āĻ $8712$

questions.question_text: A and B started a business by investing ‚āĻ 2400 and ‚āĻ 3600 respectively. At the end of 4th month from the start of the business, C joined with ‚āĻ ‚ÄėX‚Äô. After 8 months from the start of the business. B withdrew ‚āĻ 600. If C‚Äôs share is ‚āĻ 8000 in the annual profit of ‚āĻ 22500, what was the amount the C invested in the business?
questions.solution:
A invests ‚āĻ $2400$ for 12 months
B invests ‚āĻ $3600$ for 8 months
And ‚āĻ $3000$ for 4 months
C invests ‚āĻ $X$ for 8 months
Ratio of profit of A, B and C
$=$ Profit of A : Profit of B : Profit of C
$=2400	imes12:(3600	imes8)+(3000	imes4):X	imes8$
$=28800:40800:8X=3600:5100:X$
Given profit of C = ‚āĻ $8000$
and total profit of A, B and C = ‚āĻ $22,500$
‚ąī $ rac{22500}{3600+5100+X}= rac{22500}{8700+X}$
‚áí $22500X=69600000+8000X$
‚áí $14500X=69600000$ ‚áí $X$ = ‚āĻ $4800$
